creator: wollman@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Mark Felder from comment #1)
Few if any existing users will ever see the pkg-message since a "pkg upgrade" blasts a zillion of them to stdout all at once, and this particular message is far too gnomic to be meaningful in that context.  (I only noticed it because I happened to go to the upstream Web site to see what the changes were.)  No user would ever expect an upgrade of a security package to turn it into a no-op, particularly when the "workaround" is to switch to doing manually what the package was installed to do automatically.  If you're truly seriously thinking that the startup script should do nothing, may I suggest GIANT BLINKING RED ASCII-ART BANNERS to the effect that installing this package used to do something useful and now it doesn't?  (For purposes of comparison, the Debuntu package *does* install a rule that actually makes the package do something useful on startup.)

The whole point is that the proper setup to make sshguard effective should happen *automatically*, not expect the admin to hand-curate ipfw rulesets (they are probably just using one of the canned defaults).
